Text[0]: Khloe used to think snakes were awful, squirmy, scaly beasts, but thanks to those fellows from the Far East, I think I've had a change of heart!
Text[1]: Apparently, there's this one serpent─the Great White Tsuchinoko─that's almost impossible to find. Though Khloe bets with all the adventuring you do, you'll come across it sooner or later!
Text[2]: You've already crossed paths with it!? That's incredible!

Text[0]: Trials of trivia have a lengthy pedigree in the Far East. The custom began as a means for ascetic monks to test their knowledge of the world.
Text[1]: Their long, arduous pilgrimages were punctuated with such quizzes, affording them the opportunity to reflect on their learning and strive for betterment.
Text[2]: It is our belief that all of mankind should walk the path of knowledge and self-improvement. If you gain even the slightest benefit from our little event, it will have been well worth braving the seas and visiting these shores.

Text[0]: The sea here is such a marvelous color! So different to the waters back home. How I'd love to dive right in and swim to my heart's content!
Text[1]: Ah, forgive me! If you're looking to take part in our little Heavensturn event, you ought to speak to the hebi bugyo!

Text[0]: Hum hum-hummm...
Text[1]: Sorry, I was malms away. Still haven't gotten used to how busy this place is.
Text[2]: Are you here to take part in the trivia trial? You better speak with the hebi bugyo─she'll provide you with all the details.

Text[0]: By the kami, I'm famished! Once my shift is over, I'm going to feast on the finest food Limsa Lominsa has to offer!
Text[1]: Oh, perhaps you're here for our Heavensturn event? Pray head to the Aftcastle─I think it's called─and speak with my sister, the hebi bugyo!

Text[0]: Having seen the real thing, I must concede that the ancient depictions of the Great White Tsuchinoko are surprisingly accurate.
Text[1]: Especially when you consider how seldom the creature was encountered. Nonetheless, the old scrolls and inscriptions don't quite convey just how...squidgy it is.
Text[2]: That isn't to say we shouldn't be thankful for all those honest chroniclers who committed this serpent's tale to parchment over the years! Indeed, it is an honor to have helped further the legend of such a fortuitous creature!

Text[0]: The fable the delegates speak of resembles the snake-worship once prevalent in my birthplace. Of course, the people ultimately turned against their guardian serpent, and came to view me as nothing more than a malevolent beast...
Text[1]: It heartens me to see that the Great White Tsuchinoko still inspires awe and reverence. Perhaps its own abundant fortune saved it from a similar fate to mine.

Text[0]: Got some time before my next meetin'─figured I'd give this Slithery Trivia Trial a shot. Heard there's a lucky li'l somethin' waiting at the end of the quiz too.
Text[1]: Claimin' something bestows good fortune─well that's hardly somethin' you can prove, right? But folks have been lining up to get their hands on a prize!
Text[2]: Which got me thinkin'. Clearly there's a demand for lucky bits and bobs─if I get the word out that my goods magically help with money woes, or somethin' to that effect, people'll happily hand over an extra gil or two! I'll be rakin' it in!

Text[0]: Those Far Eastern rice cakes look absolutely delightful! Apparently, they're hard as pebbles, though! I was expecting something squidgier, if I'm completely honest with you...
Text[1]: I also heard about a scrumptious treat called “rice pudding.” All these rice-based delicacies─it's an exciting new frontier of dessert discovery!

Text[0]: Well met. You're speaking with the mochi daikan! I must say, it's a delight to see all the interest this event has generated!
Text[1]: And I heard all about your encounter with the Great White Tsuchinoko! How auspicious!
Text[2]: Ah, I don't suppose I can interest you in some advent cakes? They can't compete with the squidgy serpent itself when it comes to fortune, but they're a good deal luckier than your average treat, that's for sure!

Text[0]: I have something to show you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Behold this white tsuchinoko ornament I won just moments ago!
Text[1]: See how it gleams with the same splendorous white as my fur? I cannot help but feel a keen sense of affinity.
Text[2]: Suffice it to say, the ornament shall have pride of place in my humble abode. I trust you will treat your newfound friend with the same reverence.

Text[0]: Detective Hardiboiled has taken the Case of the Displaced Inspector, so for the moment we wait to see what he digs up. I'm sure there'll be plenty for us to do when the clues start to pour in!
Quite the...<chew>...selection of candidates this time...<burp>...eh?
If you feel you've pulled the wool over our eyes, good. That just shows your capacity for villainy─be proud of it.
I can see in your eyes that you've been through more than I can imagine. Hold on to your anger, and use it to bring the tyrannical rulers down to earth.
I can't imagine it will take long for news of our appreciation sale to spread all across Tural.
Text[1]: It will be a tale for the ages, and we'll all be characters within it... Ah, how I look forward to the day it reaches my ears once more!

Text[0]: Ever since we held our appreciation sale, I've received an influx of requests to write and send letters outside Tuliyollal. For me, it's an important reminder that our country is more connected than we often realize.
Text[1]: Speaking of the sale, I managed to find the most wondrous ointment for my wrists. I need only apply a small amount, and the pain of writing all but melts away!

Text[0]: Good to see you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. I'm happy to report that our Alexandrian neighbors have enjoyed my juice blends, praising their “light sweetness”!
Text[1]: Perhaps I needn't worry about competing with energy drinks after all. Why, there may even be a viable market for my products over there!

Text[0]: Juicecraft profits are swiftsoaring, so Halnaruk had better start stuffseeking for more tastyfresh!
Might be an effect of the appreciation sale, but we've been gettin' a lot more orders of late─and from all sorts of folk!
Why, hello there, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Have you happened across any impeccably crafted metalworks lately?
Text[1]: As for myself, I bought a Yok Huy stone sculpture during the event. You know what they say: “To create art, an artisan must live and breathe art!”

Text[0]: Fancy seein' you here, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! We've got some <Emphasis>finomenal</Emphasis> catches in stock today!
Text[1]: And since Pashenine's stickin' around for a while yet, I may actually manage to sell 'em!

Text[0]: Welcome, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Did you enjoy the appreciation sale while it lasted?
Text[1]: Personally, I enjoyed traveling to the outskirts to spread the word. Though it could scarcely be called a proper journey, it was still great fun, and I look forward to my next opportunity.
Text[2]: But first, perhaps I ought to raise Uvlo's wages. Like you, he is a worker of tireless dedication, so I daresay he's earned it.

Text[0]: Did you get a chance to meet the previous gatherer who worked for the apothecary? We spoke during the appreciation sale, and I was asked to “look after Shunye.”
Text[1]: Is it not passing strange for an employer to be placed in the care of his subordinate? Then again...given Shunye's sluggish disposition, I think I can see why.
Text[2]: Perhaps we Viera are not the only culture that views neighbors as family. I wonder how highly <Emphasis>I</Emphasis> happen to rank, in that case...

Text[0]: When I received Donuhanu's letter about the appreciation sale, my parents encouraged me to spend more time in Tuliyollal that I might broaden my horizons as a proud, full-fledged Whalaqee.
Text[1]: I expect there is much to learn before I return home!

Text[0]: This parasol is truly the peak of ovoid aesthetics. Timing my visit to coincide with Hatching–tide was a fine idea indeed!
When I laid eyes upon this parasol, its quality spoke loud and clear. A triumph of design, both practical and decorative. It's not often I find an article so worthy of my discernment.
To think I'd end up with one of these delightful parasols all for myself! You and your associates are so generous!

When Nonotta first relayed the awful news, I wondered how we could possibly salvage the situation. But now I can see I needn't have worried─this has eggceeded all expectations! Thank you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>!
Adventurer, I had such fun preparing the parasols with you! I'll be forever grateful for all your help!
Valentione's Day is upon us once more! During this season of love and passion, may all find the courage to bare their hearts to the objects of their affection!
We leave Laurovic in your capable hands!
Ah, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, we're given to understand there has been progress with Laurovic!
Though not the Driancoin, I had previously seen the roses grown by House Haillenarte.
Text[1]: They regularly accompany missives sent by the House, and always adorn the entrance hall of the family manor.
Text[2]: It might be said that the flower is their way of connecting with others.

Text[0]: For this year's Valentione's Day, we have roses, roses, and <Emphasis>more</Emphasis> roses! Please enjoy the festivities to the fullest─and preferably with your special someone!
This year's festivities wouldn't be possible without Laurovic's lovely roses. Do what you can for him, won't you?
We've had the story from Laurovic and Sylginie. How amazing that you could help them to find each other again!
Text[1]: It inspires me to redouble my efforts. I shall continue striving to become an emissary who can resolve all manner of quandaries of the heart!

Text[0]: My thanks again for your kind aid. This second chance with Sylginie I've been gifted, I shall make the most of!
I shall spend some time thinking on what I am able to do. But whatever may come, I will not give Laurovic cause for sorrow again.
That Hortefense... He went to the Botanists' Guild and picked the flowers himself for his bouquet for me...
Text[1]: ...N-Not that I'm impressed, mind you! I would've been perfectly happy with anything he might have offered, is what I wished to say!

Text[0]: I am but a novice in the garden, but 'tis plain Laurovic is exceptional at his craft.
Text[1]: Above all, however, he loves what he does. His roses are the most beautiful specimens, and utterly without flaw. He must care for them as though they were his children.

Text[0]: As the symbol of love, the rose is a regular fixture in romantic literature. Truly, no flower is better suited to fostering ardor and affection during Valentione's Day.
Did you know, the rose also symbolizes passion? And with the fiery passion I possess as an emissary of love, I shall do my utmost to solve everyone's dilemmas in romance!
I can't thank you enough for offering to help. If I'd known you take commissions, I'd have asked long ago!
We really owe you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. With your help, our dream of rejoining the stations is finally within grasp! Ah, it brings a tear to the eye...
Text[1]: Of course, the next step is to lay rail across the rest of the continent. Every contribution is a little more fuel in the tank for us!

Text[0]: I didn't know you were an artisan until just now, but am I ever glad to hear it!
Text[1]: ...How <Emphasis>do</Emphasis> you manage to turn up and save the day every time we're in trouble, anyhow? And you make it look so easy, too!

Text[0]: They've got some incredible technology in that dome, you know? The day you can ride a train from coast to coast might come sooner than you think!
Ah, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, it's good to see you here at the station. I was worried Nitowikwe might have a monopoly on your free time.
The journey to Yyasulani and back will be a damn sight easier once our two stations are reconnected. Time to roll up my sleeves and get to work!
Once you leave the Hall of the Unbound, you will be unable to return.
